title: Eye Context Alert Dedup: Persisted to Disk Across Hot-Swaps
desc: contextWarnedPcts is pre-populated from runtime/context_warned.json on Eye startup. SaveContextWarnedEntry writes each new max-MB. Prevents re-alerting on 60%+ JSONL after Eye hot-swap or restart.
steps: Eye stores context-alert history in runtime/context_warned.json (key=jsonlPath, value=maxMbAlerted). On startup LoadContextWarnedState() pre-populates contextWarnedPcts so hot-swapped Eye inherits the previous session's alert history.

SaveContextWarnedEntry() is called after every new alert. It only writes if new MB > previous MB (never decrements). This ensures a new session JSONL starts fresh at 0 while an old large JSONL stays deduped.

State file is in bin/wkappbot.hq/runtime/context_warned.json. Dedup is keyed by jsonlPath (lowercased, forward-slashes). To force re-alert on a JSONL, delete its key from the JSON file or delete the file entirely.
author: claude
